questions.text: Treating someone unfairly because of their background is called:
questions.explanation: Discrimination is the unfair treatment of people based on their caste, religion, gender, disability, or other characteristics.

questions.text: The Indian Constitution prohibits discrimination against citizens.
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution guarantees equality to all citizens and prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth.

questions.correctAnswer: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution and a social reformer.
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the principal architect of the Indian Constitution and a champion of Dalit rights who fought against caste discrimination.

questions.text: ________ means treating all people equally regardless of their differences.
questions.explanation: Equality means that all people are treated with equal respect and have equal opportunities, regardless of their background or characteristics.
